title: OER repo + static build + portable/LMS export + CI (lint, link-check, citation-coverage scaffold)
context: Oncology-Data-Literacy needs an OER repo and static build pipeline before course content authoring begins. The architecture is a pnpm + TypeScript/ESM repo with source content in Markdown/MDX, built into a static OER site and exported to portable formats (HTML, print PDF, and an OER-package/Common-Cartridge or SCORM export for LMS partners). CI must enforce lint, link-check, and a citation-coverage scaffold (stub that will fail on any uncited statistic once content exists). No secrets, PII, or patient data in the repo.
objective: Bootstrap the OER repository and build pipeline: pnpm + TypeScript/ESM workspace, a chosen static OER generator (e.g., Astro or Docusaurus), portable export targets (HTML, print PDF, Common Cartridge/SCORM for LMS), and CI that runs lint, link-check, and a citation-coverage scaffold stub. The scaffold should be wired so it would fail on an uncited statistic once content exists, even if no content is present yet.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm + TypeScript/ESM repo with a chosen static OER generator committed and build passing
Portable export targets wired (HTML, print-ready PDF, and at least one OER-package/LMS export format — Common Cartridge or SCORM)
CI pipeline runs lint, link-check, and a citation-coverage scaffold stub; the scaffold is structured to fail on uncited statistics when content is added
No secrets, PII, identifiable patient data, or controlled-access data in the repo; build requires no runtime secrets
Maintainer-reviewed PR is green (build, lint, link-check, citation scaffold) before merge
output: A merged PR establishing the OER repo structure, static build pipeline, portable export formats, and CI with lint + link-check + citation-coverage scaffold.
